requirements.full_sentence:
It may be noted that, whenever necessary, Reserve Bank shall issue directions to
Authorised Persons through A.P.
requirements.object: Persons through A

requirements.full_sentence:
The Master Direction issued
herewith shall be amended suitably simultaneously.

Authorised Persons shall mean Authorised Dealer Category-I banks, Standalone
Primary Dealers authorised as Authorised Dealer Category-III and for the purpose of
exchange traded currency derivatives, Recognised Stock Exchanges and Recognised
Clearing Corporations, authorised under Section 10 (1) of the FEMA, 1999.
requirements.object: banks, Standalone Primary Dealers authorised as Authorised Dealer Category-III and for the purpose of exchange trade

requirements.full_sentence:
(a) 'Anticipated exposure' means currency risk arising on account of current or
capital account transactions permissible under the FEMA, 1999 or any rules or
regulations made thereunder, that are proposed to be entered into in future.
requirements.object: account of current or capital account transactions permissible under the FEMA, 1999 or any rules or regulations made thereund

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) 'Contracted exposure' means currency risk arising on account of current or
capital account transactions permissible under the FEMA, 1999 or any rules or
regulations made thereunder, that have been entered into.
requirements.object: account of current or capital account transactions permissible under the FEMA, 1999 or any rules or regulations made thereund

requirements.full_sentence:
Directions for OTC foreign exchange transactions
Note: Money changing transactions are not in the scope of these Directions and shall
be governed by the Master Direction - Money Changing Activities dated January 01,
2016, as amended from time to time, or any other rule, regulation or Direction issued
in this regard.
requirements.object: Master Direction - Money Changing Activities dated January 01, 2016, as amended from time to time, or any other rule, regulation or

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Authorised Dealers shall classify users as retail or non-retail for the purpose of
offering foreign exchange derivative contracts and foreign currency interest rate
derivative contracts.
requirements.object: or non-retail for the purpose of offering foreign exchange derivative contracts and foreign currency interest rate derivative

requirements.full_sentence: (ii) The following users shall be eligible to be classified as non-retail users:
requirements.object: classified as non-retail users:

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Any user who is not eligible to be classified as a non-retail user shall be classified
as a retail user.
requirements.object: retail user

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Any user who is otherwise eligible to be classified as a non-retail user shall have
the option to get classified as a retail user.
requirements.object: get classified as a retail user

requirements.full_sentence:
(v) Any user who is otherwise eligible to be classified as a retail user shall have the
option to get classified as a non-retail user subject to the condition that the user makes
a request to an Authorised Dealer in this regard and the Authorised Dealer is satisfied
that the user has the risk management capabilities suitable for classification as a non-
retail user.
requirements.object: get classified as a non-retail user subject to the condition that the user makes a request to an Authorised Dealer in this regard a

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Authorised Dealers may offer the following foreign exchange contracts, involving
INR or otherwise, to users (both retail and non-retail):
requirements.object: exchange contracts, involving INR or otherwise, to users (both retail and non-retail):

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Authorised Dealers may offer foreign exchange cash, tom and spot contracts,
involving INR or otherwise, to users for permissible current or capital account
transactions.
requirements.object: tom and spot contracts, involving INR or otherwise, to users for permissible current or capital account transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Authorised Dealers may offer deliverable foreign exchange derivative contracts
involving INR to users for the purpose of hedging.
requirements.object: derivative contracts involving INR to users for the purpose of hedging

requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Authorised Dealer Category-I banks with an operating IFSC Banking Unit and
Standalone Primary Dealers authorised as Authorised Dealer Category-III may offer
NDDCs involving INR to resident users for the purpose of hedging and to non-resident
users without any restriction in terms of purpose.
requirements.object: to resident users for the purpose of hedging and to non-resident users without any restriction in terms of purpose

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Authorised Dealers may offer deliverable and non-deliverable foreign exchange
derivative contracts not involving INR to users without any restriction in terms of
purpose.
requirements.object: foreign exchange derivative contracts not involving INR to users without any restriction in terms of purpose

requirements.full_sentence:
(v) Authorised Dealers may offer foreign currency interest rate derivatives to users
without any restriction in terms of purpose.
requirements.object: rate derivatives to users without any restriction in terms of purpose

requirements.full_sentence:
(vi) Authorised Dealers may offer currency swaps to resident users, other than
individuals, for the purpose of converting their INR liability into a foreign currency
liability.
requirements.object: resident users, other than individuals, for the purpose of converting their INR liability into a foreign currency liability

conditions: In case of resident retail users
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of resident retail users, such conversion shall be subject to the
existence of a natural hedge.
requirements.object: existence of a natural hedge

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) While offering a foreign exchange derivative contract involving INR to a user, other
than NDDCs involving INR offered to non-resident users, and during the life of such
contracts, Authorised Dealers shall ensure that:

No adjustment to the hedge is required to be made if, in the considered opinion
of the Authorised Dealer, the change in exposure is not material;
requirements.object: in the considered opinion of the Authorised Dealer, the change in exposure is not material

conditions:
where the value of the exposure falls below the notional of the
derivative
requirements.full_sentence:
(d) In cases where the value of the exposure falls below the notional of the
derivative, the notional should be suitably adjusted unless such divergence
has occurred on account of changes in the market value of the exposure, in
which case the user may, at his discretion, continue with the derivative contract
till its original maturity.
requirements.object: such divergence has occurred on account of changes in the market value of the exposure, in which case the user may, at his
in the considered opinion of the Authorised Dealer, the change in exposure is not material

conditions: Where the value of the exposure is not ascertainable with certainty
requirements.full_sentence:
(e) Where the value of the exposure is not ascertainable with certainty,
derivative contracts may be booked on the basis of reasonable estimates.
requirements.object: basis of reasonable estimates

requirements.full_sentence:
Such estimates should be reviewed periodically to ensure compliance with (c)
and (d) above.
requirements.object: ensure compliance with (c) and (d) above

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that Authorised Dealers shall permit users to take position up to USD
100 million equivalent of notional value (outstanding at any point of time),
across all Authorised Dealers, for hedging contracted exposure without the
requirement to establish the existence of underlying exposure.
requirements.object: position up to USD 100 million equivalent of notional value (outstanding at any point of time), across all Authorised Dealers, fo

requirements.full_sentence:
Authorised
Dealers shall inform users that while they are not required to establish the
existence of underlying exposure, they must ensure the existence of a valid
underlying contracted exposure which has not been hedged using any other
derivative contract and should be in a position to establish the same, if
required.
requirements.object: they are not required to establish the existence of underlying exposure, they must ensure the existence of a valid underlying contracted exposure which has not been hedged using any other derivative contract and should be in a position to establish the same

requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Authorised Dealers shall allow users to freely cancel and rebook derivative
contracts.
requirements.object: cancel and rebook derivative contracts

requirements.full_sentence:
However, net gains (gains over and above losses, if any) on derivative
contracts booked to hedge an anticipated exposure shall be passed on to the eligible
user only at the time of the cash flow of the anticipated transaction.
requirements.object: the eligible user only at the time of the cash flow of the anticipated transaction

requirements.full_sentence:
In case of part
delivery, net gains shall be transferred on a pro-rata basis.
requirements.object: pro-rata basis

conditions:
if any, on
derivative contracts booked to hedge an anticipated exposure whose underlying cash
flow has not materialised
requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Authorised Dealers may, in exceptional cases, pass on the net gains, if any, on
derivative contracts booked to hedge an anticipated exposure whose underlying cash
flow has not materialised, provided the Authorised Dealer is satisfied that the absence
of cash flow is on account of factors which are beyond the control of the user.
requirements.object: pass on the net gains

requirements.full_sentence:
Such
instances along with specific justification, shall be kept on record by the Authorised
Dealer.
requirements.object: by the Authorised Dealer

requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Authorised Dealers may call for such documents from users as they deem
necessary for complying with the requirements of these Directions.
requirements.object: from users as they deem necessary for complying with the requirements of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
(v) While offering foreign exchange derivative / foreign currency interest rate
derivative to the retail user, Authorised Dealers shall provide the mid-market mark /

resident (or its central treasury / group entity, where applicable), Authorised Dealers
may deal with a non-resident user either directly or on a back-to-back basis through

subsidiaries and joint ventures of Authorised Dealers) subject to the following
conditions:
requirements.object: user either directly or on a back-to-back basis through overseas entities (including overseas branches

conditions: In case of a central treasury / group entity of the non-resident user

required by the Reserve Bank of India in respect of the aforesaid transactions
in the manner and time prescribed.

funds and / or inward remittance through normal banking channels.
requirements.object: case of non-resident users, all payables incidental to the foreign exchange transactions are met by the user out of repatriable fun

(viii) Market-makers in OTC markets shall comply with the Master Direction - Reserve
Bank of India (Market-makers in OTC Derivatives) Directions, 2021 issued vide RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
Directions for exchange traded currency derivatives
Note: Recognized Stock Exchanges shall inform users that while they are not
required to establish the existence of underlying exposure, they must ensure

hedged using any other derivative contract and should be in a position to
establish the same, if required.

requirements.full_sentence:
3.1 Recognised Stock Exchanges and Recognised Clearing Corporations shall not
deal in or otherwise undertake the business relating to foreign exchange derivatives
unless they hold an authorization issued by the Reserve Bank of India under Section
10(1) of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999.
requirements.object: undertake the business relating to foreign exchange derivatives unless they hold an authorization issued by the Reserve Bank of I

(i) Recognized Stock Exchanges may offer the following foreign exchange derivative
contracts, involving INR or otherwise, to persons resident in India and persons
resident outside India:

Recognized Stock Exchange shall ensure that:

Authorised Dealers may also purchase call or put options to hedge

part or full, owing to the contraction of the value of portfolio, it may be allowed

requirements.full_sentence:
Operational Guidelines, Terms and Conditions
a) While using products involving options, it may be ensured that there is no net
receipt of premium, either direct or implied.
requirements.object: is no net receipt of premium, either direct or implied

requirements.full_sentence: The tenor of such contracts should not exceed six months.

Hedging of Capital
Users - Foreign banks operating in India
Product - Forward foreign exchange contracts
Operational Guidelines, Terms and Conditions
a) Tier I capital -
i) The capital funds should be available in India to meet local regulatory and
CRAR requirements and, hence, these should not be parked in nostro
accounts.
requirements.object: to meet local regulatory and CRAR requirements and, hence, these should not be parked in nostro accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
Foreign currency funds accruing out of hedging should not be parked
in Nostro accounts but should remain swapped with banks in India at all times.
requirements.object: accounts but should remain swapped with banks in India at all times

requirements.full_sentence:
ii) The forward contracts should be for tenors of one or more years and may be
rolled over on maturity.
requirements.object: maturity

requirements.full_sentence:
(a) Authorised Dealers may undertake trading in all permitted exchange traded
currency derivatives within their Net Open Position Limit (NOPL) subject to limits
stipulated by the exchanges (for the purpose of risk management and preserving
market integrity) provided that any synthetic USD-INR position created using a
combination of exchange traded FCY- INR and cross-currency contracts shall have to
be within the position limit prescribed by the exchange for the USD-INR contract.
requirements.object: the position limit prescribed by the exchange for the USD-INR contract

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) Authorised Dealers may net / offset their positions in the ETCD market against
the positions in the OTC derivatives markets.
requirements.object: positions in the ETCD market against the positions in the OTC derivatives markets

requirements.full_sentence:
Keeping in view the volatility in the
foreign exchange market, Reserve Bank may however stipulate a separate sub-limit
of the NOPL (as a percentage thereof) exclusively for the OTC market as and when
required.
requirements.object: sub-limit of the NOPL (as a percentage thereof) exclusively for the OTC market as and when required

a) All categories of overseas foreign currency borrowings of AD Category-I banks,
(except for borrowings at (c) below), including existing External Commercial
Borrowings and loans/overdrafts from their Head Office, overseas branches and

subject to conditions laid down in (f) below.

Net Overnight Open Position Limit (NOOPL) for calculation of capital charge
on forex risk
NOOPL may be fixed by the boards of the respective Authorised Dealers and
communicated to the Reserve Bank immediately through CIMS / email.
requirements.object: boards of the respective Authorised Dealers and communicated to the Reserve Bank immediately through CIMS / email

requirements.full_sentence:
However, such
limits should not exceed 25 percent of the total capital (Tier I and Tier II capital) of the
Authorised Dealer.
requirements.object: the total capital (Tier I and Tier II capital) of the Authorised Dealer

requirements.full_sentence: The Net Open position may be calculated as per the method given below:
requirements.object: the method given below:

requirements.full_sentence:
Calculation of the Net Open Position in a Single Currency
The open position must first be measured separately for each foreign currency.
requirements.object: for each foreign currency

requirements.full_sentence: This should include all accrued income/expenses.

Authorised Dealers may, therefore, calculate the overall net open position as follows:
i.
requirements.object: overall net open position as follows: i

requirements.full_sentence:
All derivative transactions including forward
exchange contracts should be reported on the basis of Present Value (PV)
adjustment.
requirements.object: basis of Present Value (PV) adjustment

requirements.full_sentence:
The overall net foreign
exchange position arrived at as above must be kept within the limit approved by the
Authorised Dealers' Board.
requirements.object: limit approved by the Authorised Dealers' Board

requirements.full_sentence:
Note: Authorised Dealers should report all derivative transactions including forward
exchange contracts on the basis of PV adjustment for the purpose of calculation of the
net open position.
requirements.object: including forward exchange contracts on the basis of PV adjustment for the purpose of calculation of the net open p

requirements.full_sentence:
Authorised Dealers may select their own yield curve for the purpose
of PV adjustments.
requirements.object: curve for the purpose of PV adjustments

requirements.full_sentence:
Authorised Dealers however should have an internal policy
approved by their ALCO regarding the yield curve/(s) to be used and apply it on a
consistent basis.
requirements.object: approved by their ALCO regarding the yield curve/(s) to be used and apply it on a consistent basis

requirements.full_sentence:
Offshore exposures
For banks with overseas presence, the offshore exposures should be calculated on a
standalone basis as per the above method and should not be netted with onshore
exposures.
requirements.object: standalone basis as per the above method and should not be netted with onshore exposures

requirements.full_sentence:
The aggregate limit (on-shore + off-shore) may be termed Net Overnight
open Position (NOOP) and will be subjected to capital charge.
requirements.object: open Position (NOOP) and will be subjected to capital charge

requirements.full_sentence:
ALCO / Internal Audit Committee of the Authorised Dealers should monitor the
utilization of and adherence to the limits.
requirements.object: and adherence to the limits

requirements.full_sentence:
Authorised Dealers should also have a system in place to demonstrate, whenever
required, the various components of the NOOP as prescribed in the guidelines for
verification by Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: in place to demonstrate

requirements.full_sentence:
Transactions undertaken by Authorised Dealers till the end of business day may be
computed for calculation of Foreign Exchange Exposure Limits.
requirements.object: of Foreign Exchange Exposure Limits

requirements.full_sentence:
The transactions
undertaken after the end of business day may be taken into the positions for the next
day.
requirements.object: positions for the next day

requirements.full_sentence: The end of day time may be approved by the Authorised Dealers' Board.
requirements.object: Authorised Dealers' Board

requirements.full_sentence:
NOP-INR may be prescribed to Authorised Dealers at the discretion of the
Reserve Bank of India depending on the market conditions.
requirements.object: Dealers at the discretion of the Reserve Bank of India depending on the market conditions

requirements.full_sentence:
The NOP-INR positions may be calculated by netting off the long & short
onshore positions (as arrived at by the short hand method) plus the net INR
positions of offshore branches.
requirements.object: off the long & short onshore positions (as arrived at by the short hand method) plus the net INR positions of offshore branch

requirements.full_sentence:
As regards option position, any excesses on account of large option Greeks
during volatile market closing / revaluations may be treated as technical
breaches.
requirements.object: breaches

requirements.full_sentence:
Such breaches should also be regularized
and ratified by appropriate authorities (ALCO / Internal Audit Committee).
requirements.object: ratified by appropriate authorities (ALCO / Internal Audit Committee)

requirements.full_sentence:
AGL may be fixed by the boards of the respective Authorised Dealers and
communicated to the Reserve Bank immediately through CIMS / email.
requirements.object: boards of the respective Authorised Dealers and communicated to the Reserve Bank immediately through CIMS / email

requirements.full_sentence:
However, such
limits should not exceed 6 times the total capital (Tier I and Tier II capital) of the
Authorised Dealer.
requirements.object: total capital (Tier I and Tier II capital) of the Authorised Dealer

requirements.full_sentence:
The procedure and
calculation of the limit should be clearly documented as an internal policy and strictly
adhered to.
requirements.object: an internal policy and strictly adhered to

requirements.full_sentence:
Authorised Dealers may ensure that the reports are properly compiled on the
basis of these guidelines: The data for a particular date has to reach us by the close
of business of the following working day.
requirements.object: are properly compiled on the basis of these guidelines: The data for a particular date has to reach us by the close of busines

requirements.full_sentence:
SWAP - Only foreign exchange swaps between Authorised Dealers should be
reported under swap transactions.
requirements.object: transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
Long term swaps (both cross currency and foreign
currency-Rupee swaps) should not be included in this report.
requirements.object: report

requirements.full_sentence:
Swap transactions
should be reported only once and should not be included under either the 'spot' or
'forward' transactions.
requirements.object: and should not be included under either the 'spot' or 'forward' transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
Buy/Sell swaps should be included in the 'Purchase' side under
'Swaps' while Sell/buy swaps should figure on the 'Sale' side.
requirements.object: 'Purchase' side under 'Swaps' while Sell/buy swaps should figure on the 'Sale' side

requirements.full_sentence:
Cancellation of forwards - The amount required to be reported under cancellation
of forward contracts against purchases from merchants should be the aggregate of
cancelled forward merchant sale contracts by Authorised Dealers (adding to the
supply in the market).
requirements.object: cancelled forward merchant sale contracts by Authorised Dealers (adding to the supply in the market)

requirements.full_sentence:
On the sale side of cancelled forward contracts, aggregate of
the cancelled forward purchase contracts should be indicated (adding to the demand
in the market).
requirements.object: the demand in the market)

requirements.full_sentence:
4 'FCY/FCY' transactions - Both the legs of the transactions should be reported in the
respective columns.
requirements.object: respective columns

requirements.full_sentence:
For example in a EUR/USD purchase contract, the EUR amount
should be included in the purchase side while the USD amount should be included in
the sale side.
requirements.object: purchase side while the USD amount should be included in the sale side

requirements.full_sentence: Transactions with RBI should be included in inter-bank transactions.
requirements.object: transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
Transactions
with financial institutions other than entities authorised to deal in foreign exchange
should be included under merchant transactions.
requirements.object: transactions

requirements.full_sentence:
Foreign Currency Balances - Cash balances and investments in all foreign
currencies should be converted into US dollars and reported under this head.
requirements.object: dollars and reported under this head

requirements.full_sentence:
Net open exchange position- This should indicate the overall overnight net open
exchange position of the Authorised Dealer in Rs.
requirements.object: net open exchange position of the Authorised Dealer in Rs

requirements.full_sentence:
The net overnight open
position should be calculated on the basis of the instructions given in Annex I.
requirements.object: basis of the instructions given in Annex I

requirements.full_sentence: All hedges with rupee as one of the legs shall be reported.
In the case of option structures, the trade with highest notional amount shall be reported.
User wise data where the exposures or the hedges (based on contracted or anticipated exposure) undertaken are above USD 25 million or equivalent shall be reported.
In Part C, INR/FCY currency swaps based on Rupee Liability above USD 25 million equivalent shall be reported.
(DIR Series) Circular No.04 dated May 03, 2024

Strike Concentration Report
Maturity Buckets Strike
1 week 2 weeks 1 month 2 months 3 months > 3 months
Price
This report should be prepared for a range of 150 paise around current spot level.
requirements.object: range of 150 paise around current spot level

conditions: When the Authorised Dealer owns an option
requirements.full_sentence:
When the Authorised Dealer owns an option, the amount should
be shown as positive.

conditions: When the bank has sold an option
requirements.full_sentence:
When the bank has sold an option, the amount should be shown as
negative.
Reports may be prepared as of every Friday and sent by the following Monday.
requirements.object: every Friday and sent by the following Monday

limit26 (in
case of

requirements.full_sentence: RBI reference rate and New York closing rates on the date of report may be used for conversion purpose.
requirements.object: purpose

requirements.full_sentence:
(a) 'Currency swap' means an OTC foreign exchange derivative contract which commits
two counterparties to exchange streams of interest payments and/or principal amounts in
different currencies on specified dates over the duration of the swap at a pre-agreed
exchange rate.
requirements.object: derivative contract which commits two counterparties to exchange streams of interest payments and/or principal amounts in diff

requirements.full_sentence:
(b) 'Foreign exchange call option (European)' means an OTC / exchange traded foreign
exchange derivative contract that gives the buyer the right, but not the obligation, to buy
an agreed amount of a certain currency with another currency at a specified exchange
rate on a specified date in the future.
requirements.object: traded foreign exchange derivative contract that gives the buyer the right, but not the obligation, to buy an agreed amount of a cer

requirements.full_sentence:
(c) 'Foreign exchange call spread' means an OTC foreign exchange derivative contract
involving simultaneous purchase and sale of equal number of OTC foreign exchange call
options (European) of same expiry and different strike price.
requirements.object: derivative contract involving simultaneous purchase and sale of equal number of OTC foreign exchange call options (European) o

requirements.full_sentence:
(d) 'Covered foreign exchange call option' means a written OTC foreign exchange call
option where the writer of the option has a long position in the asset underlying the option.
requirements.object: exchange call option where the writer of the option has a long position in the asset underlying the option

requirements.full_sentence:
(e) 'Covered foreign exchange put option' means a written OTC foreign exchange put
option where the writer of the option has a short position in the asset underlying the option.
requirements.object: exchange put option where the writer of the option has a short position in the asset underlying the option

requirements.full_sentence:
(f) 'Foreign exchange forward' means an OTC foreign exchange derivative contract
involving the exchange of two currencies on a specified date in the future (more than two
business days later) at a rate agreed on the date of the contract.
requirements.object: derivative contract involving the exchange of two currencies on a specified date in the future (more than two business days la

requirements.full_sentence:
(g) 'Foreign exchange future' means an exchange traded foreign exchange derivative
contract involving the exchange of two currencies on a specified date in the future (more
than two business days later) at a rate agreed on the date of the contract, but does not
include foreign exchange forward.
requirements.object: exchange derivative contract involving the exchange of two currencies on a specified date in the future (more than two busi

requirements.full_sentence:
(h) 'Foreign exchange put option (European)' means an OTC / exchange traded foreign
exchange derivative contract that gives the buyer the right, but not the obligation, to sell
an agreed amount of a certain currency for another currency at a specified exchange rate
on a specified date in the future.
requirements.object: traded foreign exchange derivative contract that gives the buyer the right, but not the obligation, to sell an agreed amount of a ce

requirements.full_sentence:
(i) 'Foreign exchange put spread' means an OTC foreign exchange derivative contract
involving simultaneous purchase and sale of equal number of OTC foreign exchange put
options (European) of same expiry and different strike price.
requirements.object: derivative contract involving simultaneous purchase and sale of equal number of OTC foreign exchange put options (European) of

requirements.full_sentence:
(j) 'Foreign exchange swap' means an OTC foreign exchange derivative contract
involving the actual exchange of two currencies (principal amount only) on a specified
date (the near leg) and a reverse exchange of the same two currencies at a date further
in the future (the far leg), at rates agreed at the time of the contract.
requirements.object: derivative contract involving the actual exchange of two currencies (principal amount only) on a specified date (the near leg)

requirements.full_sentence:
(k) 'Forward rate agreement' means a cash-settled OTC foreign currency interest rate
derivative contract between two counterparties, in which a buyer will pay or receive, on
the settlement date, the difference between a pre-determined fixed rate (FRA rate) and a
reference interest rate, applied on a notional principal amount, for a specified forward
period.
requirements.object: currency interest rate derivative contract between two counterparties, in which a buyer will pay or receive, on the settlem

requirements.full_sentence:
(l) 'Interest rate call option (European)' means an OTC foreign currency interest rate
derivative contract that gives the buyer the right, but not the obligation, to buy an interest
rate instrument or receive an interest rate on a notional principal at a pre-determined price
/ rate on a specified date in the future.
requirements.object: interest rate derivative contract that gives the buyer the right, but not the obligation, to buy an interest rate instrument o

requirements.full_sentence:
(m) 'Interest rate cap' means a series of interest rate call options (European) (called
caplets) in which the buyer of the option receives a payment at the end of each period
when the underlying interest rate is above a rate agreed in advance.
requirements.object: rate call options (European) (called caplets) in which the buyer of the option receives a payment at the end of each period when

requirements.full_sentence:
(n) 'Interest rate collar' means an OTC foreign currency interest derivative contract where
a market participant simultaneously purchases an interest rate cap and sells an interest
rate floor on the same interest rate for the same maturity and notional principal amount.
requirements.object: interest derivative contract where a market participant simultaneously purchases an interest rate cap and sells an interest ra

requirements.full_sentence:
(o) 'Interest rate floor' means a series of interest rate put options (European) in which the
buyer of the option receives a payment at the end of each period when the underlying
interest rate is below a rate agreed in advance.
requirements.object: rate put options (European) in which the buyer of the option receives a payment at the end of each period when the underlying int

requirements.full_sentence:
(p) 'Interest rate put option (European)' means an OTC foreign currency interest rate
derivative contract that gives the buyer the right, but not the obligation, to sell an interest
rate instrument or pay an interest rate on a notional principal at a pre-determined
price/rate on a specified date in the future.
requirements.object: interest rate derivative contract that gives the buyer the right, but not the obligation, to sell an interest rate instrument

requirements.full_sentence:
(q) 'Interest rate swap' means an OTC foreign currency interest derivative contract in
which two counterparties agree to exchange one stream of future interest payments for
another, applied on a notional principal amount, over a specified period.
requirements.object: interest derivative contract in which two counterparties agree to exchange one stream of future interest payments for another,

requirements.full_sentence:
(r) 'Reverse interest rate collar' means an OTC foreign currency interest rate derivative
contract which involves simultaneous purchase of an interest rate floor and sale of an
interest rate cap on the same interest rate for the same maturity and notional principal
amount.
requirements.object: interest rate derivative contract which involves simultaneous purchase of an interest rate floor and sale of an interest rate
